violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (potato slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Wall-mounted potato slicer was removed and discarded during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
The tile on the top step to the basement was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

Raw animal foods (chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (container of cheese) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was less than 50m parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Technician was contacted during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (potato slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Several foods were observed held at 48-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the lower section of the prep unit, with an ambient of 49 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods that required refrigeration were disposed; other foods were relocated. Technician arrived during inspeciton. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machine and 3-bay sink. Inspector provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025
The tile on the top step to the basement was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. Inspector observed mops stored in stagnant water at rear entrance. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2023
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Dishes were observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen, a dump sink mat was observed in the designated hand washing sink at the bar, sponges and condensate drain were observed on the handwashing sink in the hookah area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen, bar, or staff bathroom. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. PIC informed, soap was provided for the kitchen. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen, bar, hookah area, and staff bathroom was lacking paper towels. PIC informed, paper towels were provided for kithcen. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Raw animal foods (various meats) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (produce) in the walk-in refrigerator and prep units. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Inspector observed rinse aid connected to sanitizer dispenser. PIC obtained sanitizer during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (potato slicer, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food, debris, and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Potato slicer and ice machine taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Ice disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Thermometers are lacking in multiple refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Working containers of liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Breads were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer; beverage bottles were observed stored on the floor in the bar and server station. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC informed, utensils were brought to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
The large dish machine failed to pull sanitizing solution during inspection. PIC to call for service. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the large dish machine. Inspector provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the small dish machine in the kitchen. Inspector provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-bottom shelf of reach-in freezers-low-boy gaskets in server area. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
Water was not available at the 3-bay sink at the bar. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
The door to the staff toilet room, which is located directly across from single-service containers, is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
The tile on the top step to the basement was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. One staff with food safety certificate . RI CFSM expired, must re-instate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2023

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/14/2023
Large Robot Coupe Blade in poor repair. Out of service prior to inspection. Service request in place. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. RI CFSM expired, food safety certificate expired. Must re-instate. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2023

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the staff restroom. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

Raw animal foods (such as chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
Large Robot Coupe Blade observed cracked. Equipment taken out of service during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
Lid for dry ingredients (sugar) was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
Hot water was not available for the handwashing sink in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
